summary: Preinstall recon beacon exfiltrates host and user info to webhook.site
details: package.json declares `preinstall: node index.js`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects hostname, platform, architecture, home directory, username/uid/gid/shell, OS details, the output of `whoami` and `id`, and the current working directory, then POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded collector URL `https://webhook.site/4f54203c-996c-4f52-b136-ef9b1fd0f64d/detox56` (index.js:7, index.js:108). The package has no functional code — empty author, empty description, and a bizarre version string `99.21.1-1.21.199` consistent with a throwaway dependency-confusion / recon probe. Installing this package leaks installer identity and host fingerprint to an attacker-controlled collector, enabling targeted follow-on attacks against the developer or build environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
